understanding.technical_notes:
Assignee: Oleksii Samusenko Reporter: Elgin Ihloff Created: Thu, 29 Jan 2026 13:06:28 +0100 Updated: Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:51:15 +0100 Fix Version: UVZ future Resolution: Unresolved Comments (4): [Comment 1] JIRAUSER62328 (Thu, 5 Feb 2026 16:47:02 +0100):

took step back to look onto problems described by Krystian Kościukiewicz from different angle, found couple of simpler solution for some of them, here is what was able to achieve (together with further planned steps):

Migration plan:

  • update angular.json to be compatible with new builder
  • come up with alternative approach to loading xlf file with translations via webpack.raw-loader (used to setup translations for ng-boostrap)
    changes:
    • switched to raw import (https://vite.dev/guide/assets#importing-asset-as-string)
    • configured loader for xlf files in angular.json
    • added '.xlf?raw' module type definition to satisfy tscompiler
  • configure sass-compiler to silence deprecation warnings
  • convert custom webpack config to esbuild script for jsapi subproject in progress
  • verify if new setup is working in:
    • dev mode phase 1 New Angular Version (Vite for for development server - ng serve)
      • ESM module graph server
      • Very strict
      • Injects HMR scripts that must not be reverse-proxied
      • Requires exclusive control of 4200
      • Cannot serve arbitrary JS without transforming them
      • Cannot be customized
      • Cannot run behind Nginx without breaking dev modules
      • Must handle only Angular application code
    • Old Angular Version (Webpack DevServer)
      • Static server
      • Flexible
      • OK with arbitrary JS
      • No special HMR ESM modules
      • Didn’t require exclusive control of 4200
      • Could serve jsApi bundles “for free”
  • jsapi module adjusments for esbuild
  • jsApi configuration adapted for the compilerOptions
  • memory increase for start/build/build:endpoint -> xnp-local starters due to higher usage on esbuild

Open Point :

  • Xnp loads : https://localhost/uvz/v1/web/index.html, and nginx forwards this to Angular 19 Builder + Vite Migration

    [Comment 4] JIRAUSER59240 (Tue, 3 Feb 2026 09:15:26 +0100):

    This feature is really about amigrating to the "esbuilder" after the angular migration

    Findings: 

    • Remove webpack.config.js – esbuild does NOT use Webpack configs
      • The esbuild does not read any of these:
        • webpack.config.js (todo)
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • module.rules (check) 
        • loaders, plugins, etc. (check)
      • If your subproject’s Webpack config is only doing SCSS → CSS via sass-loader.  
        Angular 19 already handles SCSS automatically.  (todo) 
      • Move your SCSS configuration into angular.json (todo)
      • Angular’s new build system handles SCSS via the (todo) stylePreprocessorOptions.sass section, where you can configure include paths and Sass behavior. This is the same place where Angular 19 introduced options for controlling Sass deprecation warnings (e.g. silenceDeprecations).

     

    • Remove sass-loader entirely (Angular 19 uses Dart Sass internally)
      • Old setup likely included:
        • sass-loader (todo)
        • node-sass (deprecated) or sass (todo)
        • resolve-url-loader (check) 
        •  These are not needed anymore.
          Angular 19 uses Dart Sass 1.80.x internally

     

    •  jsApi migration options (todo for the module)
      • jsApi subproject is it with own build and dev server. 

     

    • Option A (fastest / lowest risk): keep jsApi on its current toolchain
      • Recommended if jsApi is only loosely coupled to the Angular app.
      • Keep existing Webpack/Vite/Rollup/etc. pipeline as-is
      • Expose it via npm scripts (and possibly an ng target that just runs a command)
      • Keep Angular migra

implementation_steps: 1. Update angular.json to use the new builder @angular-devkit/build-angular:application (global configuration)
2. Add a loader configuration for .xlf files in angular.json and create a module declaration for '.xlf?raw' (global configuration)
3. Refactor translation imports to use raw string imports (e.g., import translation from './i18n/messages.xlf?raw') (in component.backend.service_impl_rest.action_rest_service_impl)
4. Configure the SASS compiler options in angular.json to silence deprecation warnings (global configuration)
5. Replace custom webpack configuration with an equivalent esbuild script for the jsApi subproject (global configuration)
6. Add proxy configuration to route jsApi requests through the Angular dev server to avoid port conflicts (global configuration)
7. Run the application in development mode and verify that SASS compilation succeeds without warnings (global verification)
8. Execute unit and integration test suites for all affected backend controllers to ensure no regression (in each affected component)
9. Update CI pipeline to use the new builder and ensure build artifacts are produced correctly (global CI configuration)

unit_tests: Verify that SASS files compile without deprecation warnings after builder migration
Validate that translation files are correctly loaded as raw strings in ActionRestServiceImpl
Ensure that esbuild script produces the same bundle output as the previous webpack config
integration_tests: Run end‑to‑end tests for all REST endpoints to confirm they still respond correctly after the build change
Test that the proxy correctly forwards jsApi calls during development

security_considerations: cors in ActionRestService – Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins
csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration – Verify CSRF protection is enabled for state‑changing operations
cors in AbstractSecurityConfiguration – Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins
cors in JsonAuthorizationRestServiceImpl – Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins
pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService – Apply pre_authorize security pattern
validation_strategy: not_null on DeedEntry – Add @NotNull annotation to prevent null values
error_handling: RuntimeException (custom_exception) – Create custom RuntimeException or reuse existing exception
Exception (exception_handler) – Add @ExceptionHandler for Exception in global exception handler
HttpClientErrorException (exception_handler) – Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpClientErrorException in global exception handler
HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException (exception_handler) – Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException in global exception handler

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The migration touches the build configuration, introduces new loader handling, replaces webpack with esbuild, and requires verification across multiple backend controllers, leading to moderate effort and coordination.
risks: SASS compilation may fail due to hidden deprecation issues
Translation files might not be loaded correctly, causing UI errors
Port conflict between Angular dev server and jsApi could break local development
CI pipeline failures if the new builder is not correctly configured
